metadata.title: Solution Architecture Document — Stellar Internal Developer Platform

documentControl.purpose: Describe the architecture of Stellar Engineering's Internal Developer Platform (IDP) — a Backstage-based developer portal and the platform services it composes (golden paths, paved roads, cost insights, scorecards) — designed to reduce cognitive load on stream-aligned teams and shorten lead time for change.
documentControl.scope: Backstage portal, platform services (Scaffolder, TechDocs, scorecards, cost insights), the underlying Kubernetes platform-as-a-service, and supporting tooling (Tekton, ArgoCD, Vault, Datadog integration). Excludes the workloads built on the platform.

executiveSummary.solutionOverview: The Stellar Platform is an internal developer platform serving ~340 engineers across 28 stream-aligned teams at Stellar Engineering Ltd, a B2B SaaS company. It centres on a Backstage developer portal that catalogues services, owners, runbooks, and dashboards; provides Software Templates (Scaffolder) for golden-path service creation; and surfaces scorecards for production readiness, security, cost, and reliability. The portal composes platform capabilities including a paved-road Kubernetes runtime (EKS + GKE), Tekton CI/CD with signed artefacts, ArgoCD GitOps, Vault-managed secrets, and unified Datadog observability.

businessContext.description: Lead time for changes stretched from 2 days to 9 days; new service bootstrapping takes 3-6 weeks across SRE/Security/Platform
Product teams carry too much accidental responsibility (clusters, pipelines, IAM, alerting) instead of customer value
14 different CI patterns, 6 Terraform module styles, 4 K8s deployment approaches, 3 observability stacks across teams
Change failure rate at 18% (DORA high-performer threshold 15%); incidents rooted in drift and unclear ownership
Inconsistent supply-chain controls and secret handling; SOC 2 Type II audit findings
Cloud spend grew 42% YoY against 18% revenue growth; no unified FinOps view

inScope: Backstage developer portal and first-party plugins
Software templates (golden paths) for net-new services
Tekton CI/CD pipelines with signed artefacts (cosign + SLSA L3)
HashiCorp Vault federation for workload secrets
Service ownership and on-call routing via Backstage
outOfScope: Replacing Datadog APM, synthetics, or on-call routing
Replacing GitHub Actions for source-repo-level checks
executiveSummary.currentState: Each team operates independently. 14 CI patterns, 6 Terraform module styles, 4 K8s deployment approaches, 3 observability stacks. New service bootstrap involves 3-6 weeks of coordination. Configuration drift common; runbooks fragmented across team wikis.

keyDecisions.rationale: CNCF graduated; vibrant plugin ecosystem; team familiarity
keyDecisions.implication: Backstage upgrade cadence becomes platform team responsibility

keyDecisions.decision: Tekton over GitHub Actions for build pipelines
keyDecisions.rationale: Native Kubernetes; signed artefacts (SLSA L3) align with security audit findings
keyDecisions.implication: Two-tier CI: GHA for source checks, Tekton for build/sign/publish

keyDecisions.rationale: Pull-based deployment improves security; declarative state visible in Git
keyDecisions.implication: All deployments via Git PRs; rollback via revert

keyDecisions.rationale: Eliminates static secrets; aligns with SOC 2 remediation

compliance.regulatedActivityDetails: The platform is a supporting system but not directly customer-facing.

regulatoryRequirements.name: SOC 2 Type II
regulatoryRequirements.impact: Supply chain, change control, access control evidence

regulatoryRequirements.name: UK GDPR
regulatoryRequirements.applicability: Limited — platform metadata only, no customer PII
regulatoryRequirements.impact: Engineer identity from Okta treated as personal data under DPA

designPatterns.rationale: Backstage's plugin model gives modular boundaries within a single deployable
Catalogue change events drive scorecard re-evaluation and notifications
Cloudflare in front of Backstage for global edge and Okta integration
Vault Agent sidecar in workloads for federated secret retrieval

externalIntegrations.purpose: Source repo metadata, PR creation by Scaffolder, code search, ownership
User authentication and SCIM-provisioned groups for RBAC
Cost telemetry by team and service
Service health, on-call rota, incident links
Workload identity federation; signing keys; deploy credentials
GitOps source of truth; manifest pull

dataView.dataSovereigntyDetails: Platform-internal metadata only; no customer data; engineer PII (name, email) limited and held in EEA-permissible regions

constraints.impactOnDesign: Pipeline templates and manifests must be cloud-agnostic where reasonable; cluster federation required

constraints.constraint: Must integrate with existing corporate IdP (Okta), Datadog, Vault, Snowflake
constraints.impactOnDesign: Replacement of any of these is out of scope
Auditable change control, access boundaries, supply chain controls

assumptions.assumption: Backstage upstream remains active and CNCF-governed
assumptions.impactIfFalse: Platform team takes greater fork maintenance burden

assumptions.assumption: Stream-aligned teams will adopt golden paths once published
assumptions.impactIfFalse: Adoption rate falls; ROI delayed; fragmentation persists

risks.riskEvent: Low team adoption of golden paths and Backstage scorecards
risks.mitigationPlan: Pilot with 3 friendly teams; embedded platform engineers; quarterly DORA review; UX research; carrot-not-stick adoption

risks.riskEvent: Vault outage disrupts deployments and workload secret access
risks.mitigationPlan: Active-passive Vault HA across two AZs; performance-replica; cached short-lived credentials minimise on-path dependency

risks.riskEvent: Platform becomes a bottleneck for product teams
risks.mitigationPlan: Self-service first principle; clear escalation paths; explicit platform SLOs (golden-path PR merge < 24h)

risks.riskEvent: Supply chain attack via signed artefact bypass
risks.mitigationPlan: Cosign + SLSA L3 enforced; Fulcio short-lived certs; admission webhooks reject unsigned images; quarterly red-team test
Quarterly platform retrospective; willingness to consolidate to single cloud if data shows complexity tax > benefit

glossary.definition: Sigstore signing tool for container images and artefacts
DevOps Research & Assessment — software delivery performance metrics
Opinionated, supported route for accomplishing a common task on the platform
Standard, well-supported solution that takes the easy path
Backstage Software Templates feature for creating new components from golden paths
Automated evaluation of services against criteria
Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts — security framework
